title: physocarpus bracteatus
intro: Physocarpus bracteatus, also known as ninebark, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its exfoliating bark and white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Physocarpus bracteatus lie in its roots and bark, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and infections, as well as promote wound healing and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Physocarpus bracteatus is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to reap its health benefits, including relieving menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
